appTagline: Create, edit and play your card games
welcomeSubtitle: Log in or create an account to continue
ForgotPasswordFormSubtitle: You will receive a recovery code in your mailbox once the form is submitted.
legalNotice: By logging in, you accept our
password_recovery_info: You will receive a recovery code in your inbox once the form is submitted.
errorLength: You must enter at least {{length}} characters
searchGame: Find game with name, description or type...
selectGameSubtitle: Choose the game you want to configure and manage. You will be able to modify its settings, create cards, and start games.
wantToSwitchGameToVisible: Would you like to make this game publicly visible on the CARD Games interface?
publicVisibilityHelperText: The game will appear in the list of available games for all users. Otherwise, the game will not be listed in the public interface. It will only be accessible to users who possess and enter a unique access code.
commentAreCollectOnGame: Comments are collected directly from the gaming platform
playerConfiguration_hint: Configure the number and conditions of the players
allowSpectators_description: Allow users to join the game as spectators. When joining a game via its ID, users will be able to choose their status (Player or Spectator).
soloMode_description: Enable this mode to play alone. This prevents users from joining as visitors or spectators for this instance.
playerParticipation_description: Check this option to allow other players to join the game. If Solo Mode is selected: Players joining the game will be automatically designated as Spectators. Otherwise, players joining the game will become Active Participants.
unsavedChangesWarning: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave this page?
roleManagementDescription: Definition and Allocation: Roles allow you to assign customized functions and powers to each player in the game. Roles with Specific Powers: Used in games where certain participants possess unique abilities (Example: The different roles in Werewolf). Structural Game Roles: Used to define responsibilities essential to the game mechanics (Example: The Dealer in Blackjack).
rewardsDefinitionDescription: Earnings Definition: This section allows you to set up the rewards players will receive following a specific action, an in-game event, or the resolution of a round. Currency Example: In Poker, chips are distributed as winnings after each successful round.
assetLibraryDescription: Explore and download resources for your cards
eventDescription: All events that are not global and are triggered by a demon or an action. This excludes events with specific values. It only includes events with fixed values that repeat.
demonDescription: A demon monitors the game and triggers based on a specific condition. It can act at any time during the game. For example, it could remove points from a player when they hold a specific card or distribute all gains at the start of the game.
withValueEventDescription: All events that require a specific value, such as input from a player or a number determined by the current situation.
eventsAndTriggersManagementDescription: This section allows you to manage everything related to game actions, whether it's for distributing cards, changing variables, or incrementing turns; it is the core of the game's mechanics.
eventConfigurationDescription: Define the event name and type
triggerConditionPlaceholder: Describe when this event should trigger...
eventActiveDescription: The event can trigger during the game
triggeredActionsDescription: Define what happens when the event is triggered
activationConditionDescription: Before activating an event, we will verify if this condition can be fulfilled
removeAllAtachedEventsForTour: Remove all attached events for tour
allPlayersHasPlayed/endOfTour: Have all players played (end of turn) ?
victory-trigger-description: This event automatically triggers victory for the specified people
features-description: You can compare elements, add conditions, and perform calculations
